_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices set at 100 in 2015, currently stands at 141, reflecting a 41% increase since 2015. This rise represents a significant overall price movement, but it is important to note that the climb has been uneven across commodities and time periods. The index aggregates diverse commodities, so changes in individual prices impact the basket variably. The recent climbs highlight heightened volatility in these soft commodity markets.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the largest increase, up 99% since 2015, nearly doubling in price. Orange juice prices rose by 34%, wheat by 19%, and sugar by 13%. These substantial increases are primarily driven by specific weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought in coffee-growing regions and citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice production. Given that soft commodities are thin markets with limited supply elasticity, such localized shocks significantly influence global prices rather than broad-based inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is largely attributable to a few weather-exposed crops experiencing supply challenges. Because these commodities are vulnerable to environmental factors, price inputs can remain elevated for extended periods as production adjusts. Understanding this dynamic is key for interpreting commodity price movements in soft markets.
